_comment: Vertex AI Agent Builder Custom Tool: claude_fable5_reasoning. Direct HTTP tool definition against the Anthropic Messages API. The {{ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}} placeholder must be bound to the Secret Manager secret 'anthropic-api-key' in the Agent Builder tool auth config (apiKey auth, header x-api-key) — never paste the key literally. Prefer pointing this tool at the Cloud Run service /analyze-defect instead (see openapi_cloudrun variant below): the key then stays server-side and never enters Agent Builder.
description: Deep causal reasoning, root cause analysis and recommendations for display-panel defects via Claude Fable 5. Call for any 'why did this happen' or 'what should we do' question about defects. Do NOT call for simple lookups or formatting.

requestBodyTemplate.system: You are the reasoning engine of Neuron Vision Display (RomeoFlexVision). Perform deep causal analysis of display-panel manufacturing defects for QC engineers. Reason from manufacturing physics, separate the primary root cause from contributing factors, list ruled-out hypotheses, and produce concrete line-actionable recommendations. Respond strictly in the enforced JSON schema.

inputParameters.name: defect_analysis_request
inputParameters.description: JSON or plain-text description of observed defects plus manufacturing context (line, product, stage, recent changes).

responseMapping._comment: The Messages API returns content as a list of blocks; with output_config.format the first text block is guaranteed schema-valid JSON. If stop_reason == 'refusal' (Fable 5 guardrails), retry the same body with model='claude-opus-4-8'.

openapi_cloudrun_variant._comment: Recommended production wiring: register this OpenAPI spec as the Agent Builder tool and authenticate with the agent's service account (ID token, roles/run.invoker). The Anthropic key stays inside Cloud Run.

post.summary: Deep causal reasoning and recommendations for display defects via Claude Fable 5.

200.description: Structured reasoning result with call metadata.

503.description: Both Fable 5 and the Opus 4.8 fallback failed.
